entry.title: Re: Implementation of Restrictions for Tyre and Battery I...
entry.content: Thanks for taking the time to understand our scenario and providing a detailed solution. I think your approach makes sense - creating a custom class for vehicle configuration, assigning characteristics for tyres and batteries, and implementing a custom mapping table and logic for installation checks. for installation checks.

entry.title: Re: Can't access my tutorials for my SAP S/4HANA sourcing and procurement public edition
entry.content:
Please don't post image, post text to make your question (and answer, hopefully) searchable: "no trainer on server checked in", "UE_TRAINER_NOT_FOUND".

Hopefully, there is an existing answer in the Web: "Ressources have to be synchronized to Manager."

entry.title: UomEntry in ITM12 is created with not aspected value
entry.content:
In UGP1 I have a group with code = 1, and 5 unit of measure

With this code, I create a new Item

 

Items item = ((Items)(myCompany.GetBusinessObject(BoObjectTypes.oItems)));

item.ItemCode = "NewCode";
item.ItemName = "Item NewCode";
item.UoMGroupEntry = 1;
item.UnitOfMeasurements.UoMType = ItemUoMTypeEnum.iutInventory;
item.UnitOfMeasurements.UoMEntry = 5;
int retCode = item.Add();
if (retCode != 0)
{
    string retStr;
    myCompany.GetLastError(out retCode, out retStr);
    Assert.Fail("Item record not insert: " + retStr);
}
Assert.AreEqual(0, retCode);

System.Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal.ReleaseComObject(item);
System.Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal.FinalReleaseComObject(item);

 

When I try to excecute this code, in OITM I found a new record with ItemCode = "NewCode", but the value in ITM12 on the field UomEntry is 1 (not 5).

Why is the UomEntry field not valued at 5, as required by my code?

 

Thanks

Riccardo

entry.title: Re: Taking Groups to the Next Level with Application-Specific Groups
entry.content:
Is this feature supported for ABAP OnPrem as a source?
Example scenario

ABAP OnPrem as a source, Local Identity as a target. App type SAP on-premise solution - eg. for SAML access to the OnPrem Fiori apps.

Currently I'm getting an error if I'm trying to ad ips.application.id to OnPrem ABAP source system.

 

ips.application.id can be added to Identity Directory (source) without any problems.

entry.title: we want profit center wise customer Ledger
entry.content:
In our Business, we had multiple profit center for sale of products. As per Business requirement, need profit center wise Customer Ledger. In customer Ledger, Business require  profit center wise opening balance, debit and credit transaction and closing balance. In table KNC1, balance stored without profit center.

Please advise

Regards

Rajesh

there is a docker image builder extension available in BAS.

see https://help.sap.com/docs/bas/sap-business-application-studio/additional-extensions

entry.title: Re: RFC call failed: Error when opening an RFC connection (CPIC-CALL: 'ThSAPOCMINIT', communicat
entry.content: in RFC Connection, the Target Host name mentioned is not correct, once check it, than it successfully working.

entry.title: Re: Defining Business Architecture: A Guide for Estimating Transformation Project
entry.content:
@Rajan  Excellent insights! The need for a clear business architecture is crucial for any ERP transformation, and I like how you emphasized the role of BCM and BPM in reducing uncertainty. It’s a reminder that successful transformations require not just new systems, but a solid understanding of the processes behind them. 

I have a couple of questions:

  • In your experience, what’s the best way to overcome resistance from business users (involving all levels of the organization) when it comes to documenting processes?
  • What’s the best way to manage process changes during the project to avoid scope creep?

Can you share any tips!

entry.title: Re: Error : Production Order change status : CLOSE
entry.content: Thanks for the advice, but my system didn't have approval process. I found error message on log : Business Object Event: GetByKey failed, err number is -2028 # # TID=7716 _BusinessObjectMethods.h 34

entry.title: Re: February 2025 Mobile App Updates question
entry.content:
Hi @vaivabaublyte,

How exactly users sign in again depends on configuration. If you want to test it out right now, you can manually sign out on the Settings screen, close the app entirely, and launch the app. This will manually force the app into the scenario that will automatically happen when the app is updated.

You can read more in this blog post, including Q&A in the comments.

Thanks!
Jason

entry.title: Re: Production Planning in a cross Plant/Company set-up
entry.content:
Hello Amgangal,

Thank you for your comment.

I’m not entirely clear on this part: “Any shortages are supplied from the factory - plant 3000, company code 3000.” Could you clarify this with a real-life example? Once I understand the scenario better, I’ll be happy to help you troubleshoot the issue further.

Looking forward to your response!

entry.title: How to download and install cloud connector in SAP BTP?
#abap_trial: Container does not start properly
entry.content:
Hello,

I try to start the container with the command

docker run --stop-timeout 3600 -i --name a4h -h vhcala4hci -p 3200:3200 -p 3300:3300 -p 8443:8443 -p 30213:30213 -p 50000:50000 -p 50001:50001 sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRIAL_2022_SP01 -skip-limits-check -agree-to-sap-license

The container is starting and the initialization begins, but eventually i get this output:

Worker Processes: starting

25.01.2025 16:43:48
Start
OK

25.01.2025 16:53:49
StartWait
FAIL: Timeout
Worker Processes: the start command failed, exit code=2
The service Worker Processes has failed to start and initialization is blocked until the problem is resolved
You can try your luck 10 times until initialization is terminated
Hint: docker containers can be joined by: docker exec -it <name> bash
Hint: HDB license must not be expired
Hint: HDB must be running
Hint: AS ABAP license must be valid
Hit Return to continue

What can i try to fix this?

 

#abap_trial

entry.title: Re: Connect SAP HANA On-Premise to SAP HANA Cloud and Database with Cloud Connector
entry.content:
The following tutorial provides another example of similar steps and includes the SQL to create remote sources and virtual tables.  
https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/hana-dbx-remote-sources.html  
Regards,
Dan

entry.title: Re: I created the SAP GUI AI Agent
entry.content:
Hi,

No, I still haven't published this project anywhere yet. I have a few ideas for its development and for changing the approach, but here, SAP somewhat disrupted my plans due to the lack of support for PyRFC.

However, I am very eager to test the operation of Agenda with other models, and I plan to use DeepSeek, but for now, there is also a lack of time for that, as I am currently developing another project. However, I will definitely want to come back to this. Especially since I also promised a summary of the costs and a deeper look into how it works from the inside.

BR

entry.title: Re: Xblnr_alt update via function module or IRBM no field...
entry.content:
Actually SAP will try to prevent you to update this fiedl (it's expected this field will automatically contain the ODN, original document number, in some case, countries)

  • Read 3399198 - BKPF-XBLNR_ALT not editable in FB02 

You could try to use other fields

  • Look for available XREF* fields such as XREF1_HD, XREF2_HD, or even BKTXT,
  • Some long text at header level
  • Any appended ZZ-fields, but in last case some substitution and/or BAdI/BTE implementations would be required

entry.title: Re: Xblnr_alt update via function module or IRBM no field for malaysia
entry.content:
Why didn't you use standard SAP solution?

  • (blog) Electronic Invoicing in Malaysia  
  • 3459968 - Electronic Invoice Processing for Malaysia: Pre-Implementation Guidance

entry.title: The Future of AI Agents in Sales: Maximizing Incentives and Payouts
entry.content:
In the ever-evolving landscape of sales, achieving targets and maximizing incentives is a top priority for sales executives. The integration of AI agents and advanced tools like SAP Incentive Management is revolutionizing how sales teams operate, providing real-time insights and optimizing performance. Let's explore how AI can benefit sales executives in achieving their compensation goals and the potential of building side-by-side extensions for tracking incentives.

1. Real-Time Insights and Predictive Analytics

AI agents can analyze vast amounts of data to provide real-time insights into sales performance. By leveraging predictive analytics, AI can forecast sales trends, identify high-potential leads, and recommend strategies to close deals faster. This enables sales executives to focus their efforts on the most promising opportunities, ultimately increasing their chances of achieving targets and earning higher incentives

2. Optimizing Sales Strategies with SAP Incentive Management

SAP Incentive Management tools, such as SAP SuccessFactors, offer comprehensive solutions for designing and managing incentive compensation plans. These tools use AI to model, compare, and optimize complex sales plans, ensuring that incentives are aligned with business goals. Real-time payment and performance insights help sales executives stay motivated and focused on their targets

3. AI-Powered Sales Coaching and Training

AI agents can provide personalized coaching and training to sales executives by analyzing their interactions with customers. Tools like AI sales agents can simulate sales scenarios, offer real-time feedback, and suggest improvements. This continuous learning process helps sales executives refine their skills, improve their performance, and achieve better results

4. Building Extensions for Incentive Tracking using SAP BTP

Developing side-by-side extensions for tracking sales incentives can further enhance the capabilities of AI agents. These extensions can integrate with existing CRM systems and SAP Incentive Management tools to provide a seamless experience. Sales executives can easily monitor their progress, view real-time updates on their incentives, and raise disputes efficiently. This transparency and accessibility empower sales teams to stay on top of their performance and compensation

5. Leveraging SAP Joule and RAG for Enhanced Performance

SAP Joule and RAG (Reterival Augumented and Generation) are powerful tools that can be integrated with AI agents to enhance sales performance. SAP Joule provides advanced analytics and insights, helping sales executives make data-driven decisions. RAG ensures compliance and accuracy in incentive calculations, reducing the risk of payment errors and disputes

 

6. AI-Driven Personalization and Customer Engagement

AI agents can personalize customer interactions by analyzing customer data and preferences. This enables sales executives to tailor their pitches and offers to meet the specific needs of each customer. Personalized engagement not only increases the likelihood of closing deals but also enhances customer satisfaction and loyalty

 

Disclaimer:

The images featured in this blog post were generated using artificial intelligence (AI) technology. While we strive to ensure the accuracy and appropriateness of these images, they are created by an automated system and may not always perfectly represent the intended subject matter. Any resemblance to real persons, living or dead, or actual events is purely coincidental. Please use these images responsibly and consider the context in which they are used.

Conclusion

The future of AI agents in sales is bright, with immense potential to transform how sales executives achieve their compensation goals. By providing real-time insights, optimizing sales strategies, offering personalized coaching, and enabling seamless incentive tracking, AI agents and tools like SAP Incentive Management are set to revolutionize the sales landscape. Embracing these technologies will empower sales teams to achieve more incentives, drive business growth, and stay ahead in the competitive market.

entry.title: Re: Create/send a file to FTP server via ABAP cloud or use SAP CPI

I am getting answers like maximum 20 character and maximum 70 character.

So, which one is correct? 

Thanks in advance.

Best Regard,

Praveen S

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: @MRobalinho Thank you for your wishes

entry.title: Re: FS Items and GL Account Mapping Table into SAC
entry.content:
Hi, 

FS Item is aggregated view of GL account. 

Usual design in SAC for this case is to have 1 dim for GL account, 1 dim for FS item. GL account dim has attribute (mapping to FS Item). 

So, for your case mapping will be in GL Account dimension

entry.title: Managed RAP Remote Service Based CAPM App: Gateway HTTP Method POST Error: 406 Not Acceptable
entry.content:
Hello Experts

I’m facing an issue with my custom Managed RAP OData V4 service integrated with a Node.js CAPM-based custom Fiori app. The app is a read-only report running in the SAP BTP environment.

To address payload query length issues, we have enabled the POST operation, and token handling for the remote service is configured as follows:

Remote service CSRF-token handling 

To add POST support for this CDS-based root entity, I implemented a Behavior Definition to handle the create operation with Behavior Pool Class (with minimal code). 

Now service metadata shows the supported formats.

However, the payload, upon reaching the backend S/4HANA system, results in the following error:

 

 

 

Error: "The resource identified by the request is only capable of generating response entities which have content characteristics not acceptable according to the accept headers sent in the request."

 

 

 

Despite the metadata correctly showing application/json as a supported format, the error persists. Simplifying the scenario, a single GET request (outside of the batch request) with Accept: application/json works perfectly. However, the issue arises specifically with the batching mechanism for the POST operation.

I came across @Andre_Fischers blog on Function Import, which discusses this concept, but I am unclear on how to apply it within the RAP framework.

How can I address the issue with the batching mechanism for POST requests? Is there something specific in the RAP framework or the request construction that I might be overlooking?

Any insights, examples, or suggestions would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance for your support!

NW ABAP Gateway (OData) ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model Node.js SAP Fiori Elements  SAP Cloud Application Programming Model

entry.title: Replication flows failure notifications
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

We are exploring options to receive notifications whenever a replication flow encounters an error in any of its objects or the entire flow, similar to the notifications available for task chains.

Any insights or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

SAP Datasphere

entry.title: Re: OData service for Fiori Analytical Table - sap.ui.tab...
entry.content: Thank you very much Gregor, it is really helpful. Best regards!

entry.title: SAP ATTP. SSCC/SGTIN hierarchy as text and new select options. Idea and steps towards realization
entry.content:
Intro.

Transaction /STTP/Cockpit > Repository Browser > Serialized Containers > Object Hierarchy

The routine daily work of an SAP consultant often includes extracting hierarchies as text. This is necessary for customs declarations, manual EPCIS events, and many other purposes. For example, you might want to extract all SSCC boxes on an SSCC pallet or all SGTINs in an SSCC box (marked as 1 and 2 on the screenshot). However, this cannot be done directly in the transaction /STTP/Cockpit.

Missing but Essential Features

Another must-have feature that is currently missing is the ability to accept multiple inputs in the Transaction /STTP/Cockpit > Repository Browser > Lots, Serialized Trade Items, Serialized Containers, etc. (marked as 1 on the screenshot).

Proposed Solution

Idea

A custom report (and transaction) that supports the following inputs:

  • S_SSCC - SSCC (multiple input supported)

  • S_GTIN - GTIN (multiple input supported)

  • S_SGTIN - SGTIN (multiple input supported)

  • S_LOTNO - Lot number (multiple input supported)

  • S_DOCS - Document numbers (multiple input supported)

Input Options

  • P_EXACT - (Strictly SSCC boxes from the list. Behavior: If checked, the output includes information only about SSCCs in the input. If unchecked, information about all SSCC boxes on the SSCC pallet is included.)

  • P_EWM_F - (Behavior: If checked, the output is without symbols "()".)

  • P_SGTIN - (Behavior: If checked, the output hierarchy is SSCC pallet > SSCC box > SGTINs. If unchecked, the output hierarchy is SSCC pallet > SSCC box.)

  • P_NBRCT - (Behavior: If checked, the output is without segments: (00), (21). If unchecked, the output includes segments (00), (21).)

Output Options

  • P_CONT - Read hierarchy

  • P_STAT - Read statuses

  • P_DOCS - Read assigned documents

For all options, the output is an ALV report with Excel export functionality.

Output for Hierarchy

ALV List: SSCC Pallet, SSCC Box, GS1 GTIN, Lot Number, Qty SGTIN, Trade Item Description

Output for Assigned Statuses

ALV List: Object Code (EPC), Logistic Status, Pack Status, Disposition

Output for Assigned Documents

ALV List: SSCC Pallet, SSCC Box, Assigned Doc. Num

Export to a spreadsheet is available for all outputs (marked as 1 on the screenshot), and MS Excel (*.xlsx) format is also supported (marked as 2 on the screenshot).

Steps Toward Realization

  1. TYPES
    If you are not familiar with data types in the /STTP/ namespace, this collection of types will be a good starting point:
    TYPES.abap

  2. FORM HANDLER
    The START-OF-SELECTION event is raised by the ABAP runtime environment during the execution of an executable program after any standard selection screens have been processed:
     START-OF-SELECTION.abap

  3. DATA MINING
    The class /sttp/cl_dm_query is a central point for custom methods for ATTP data mining:
     ATTP_MISC_CLASS_METHODS

Conclusion

A useful custom report has been introduced, which can significantly aid the routine work of SAP ATTP consultants. If the described use case aligns with your system requirements, you can implement this report in your own system. The UI input/output options and steps for realization have been described in detail.

entry.title: Issues with creating Signup page
entry.content: Hello everyone, I am trying to create a Signup page for my app, I followed closely some videos I saw on youtube as well as do some reading around, just to fix it but I dont seem to see where the problem is. I have been stuck and I believe assistance from anyone at this point might help. Thank you

entry.title: Re: Sending mail from C4C when creating Email via OData
entry.content:
I know it's been a while, but in case someone stumbles across this, I had the same requirement. The issue is, that the Email is not send, because the action to send it is not executed in a create via oData. 

In order to make it work a small enhancement via SDK is possible. The send action is callable from the activity BO. 

So extend the activity BO and in the before save check if, it is a create, the user is the oData user and it is activity type. If the conditions are true the send action can be called.

BR

David

entry.title: What is the correct course for S/4 HANA public cloud project system or EPPM
entry.content:
What is the correct course for S/4 HANA public cloud project system or EPPM? I could only find one i.e. professional services. As per my understanding this is an industry specific learning.

Any inputs would be a great help.

entry.title: Re: Herencia de roles/derive roles
entry.content: Debes verificar que el rol padre este conforme, luego que generas los hijos actualizas el perfil del rol padre hacia todos los hijos hay una opción en la PFCG, cada vez que ejecutes alguna modificación la debes hacer en el padre.

entry.title: Re: I need to associate my previous certification with my...
entry.content:
SAP Support Portal – Try logging into the SAP Support Portal (support.sap.com) using any credentials you remember. If you have multiple S-User IDs, your account may be linked to more than one.

Contact SAP Support – If you still cannot retrieve it, contact SAP support by raising a request through the SAP Customer Interaction Center (CIC). They can help you recover your S-User ID based on your previous company details.

entry.title: Re: Post a 501 movement type using BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE
entry.content: Thank you @Harsh14 for your response.

entry.title: Re: How to download and install cloud connector in SAP BTP?
entry.content: You don't install cloud connector directly in BTP. Cloud Connector is to allow cloud applications in BTP to connect to SAP and/or other systems/applications inside a companies internal network.

entry.title: Re: How to download and install cloud connector in SAP BTP?
entry.content:
I have completed the setup of the Cloud Connector by following SAP's documentation here: https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/cloud-connector
The download link for the Cloud Connector is here: https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/#cloud

If you want to create another with the same email you will have the message error.

entry.title: Re: How to setup SAP CDC Solution in Azure Data Factory
entry.content:
Dear Experts, need your guidance on SAP Note 3255746 to prohibit the use of ODP API for 3rd party applications. Assuming this change may impact ability to ingest data from SAP to Azure DataFactory,  SAP Note 3255746: Unpermitted usage of ODP

Q 1- Is there any alternate solution/connector to pull the data from SAP source systems (ECC, S/4HANA), non-SAP systems and connect to Azure DataFactory?

Q 2- Any authentic SAP note/solution approach from SAP for, as the note # 3255746 shows it's a risk to use ODP for connecting the Azure DataFactory. 

Q3- Please share your guidance if there are any performance issues with alternate SAP solution (if available).

Appreciate your quick guidance & support, thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: What is the correct course for S/4 HANA public cloud...
entry.content:
Hi,

I guess you are correct as of now Professional Services is the only Public cloud course offering available for SAP Project Management related learning.

Regards

Basva

entry.title: Re: Managing User Roles Directly in Azure
entry.content:
Yes, you can assign AD / AzureAD groups. The group assignments of the user are transmitted to BTP in the SAML token. On BTP side you can then use Role Collection Mapping to assign BTP role collections to every user that is part of a specific AzureAD group.

Attention: The SAML token is limited in size, therefore if the number of AD groups gets very high, this comes to a limit. Also consider process-related dependencies within your organization (whenever the BTP Team creates a new role collection, they must ask the AD / Azure Team to create a new Group)

entry.title: Build Apps - Add Integration - BTP Destinations - Fetching services for catalog failed error
entry.content:

I have set up the SAP cloud connector configuration and added Destination in my BTP trial account. Both the connections were successful. When I goto Build apps  and try to Add Integration - BTP destinations, I'm getting below error.

Fetching services for catalog XXX failed:Request failed with status code 400  (XXX - Destination name)

What could be missing here.

Regards,

Gautham.

entry.title: Outbound process, Picking by Warehouse order using RFUI
entry.content:
Outbound Process:

The outbound process in SAP EWM involves picking, packing, and shipping goods to fulfill customer orders. It begins with creating outbound deliveries and ends with goods issue, confirming that items have been dispatched. SAP EWM helps optimize the entire process by managing warehouse tasks efficiently.

Radio Frequency User Interface:

The Radio Frequency (RF) user interface provides warehouse workers with a mobile, handheld device to interact with the system in real-time. It enables efficient execution of tasks like picking, packing, and confirming activities with minimal manual effort. RF devices increase accuracy and speed by providing direct communication with the warehouse management system.

Picking:

Picking is the process of retrieving items from storage locations based on a customer order or warehouse request. The system can generate picking tasks and assign them to warehouse operators using RF devices, ensuring the correct quantity and items are picked. Various picking strategies (e.g., single or multi-order picking) can be configured for optimal efficiency.

Warehouse Order:

A warehouse order is a collection of tasks grouped together to optimize the warehouse worker's efficiency. It can include tasks for picking, packing, and other related activities. Warehouse orders streamline the execution process by organizing tasks into manageable units, helping to ensure faster and more accurate order fulfillment.

 

Steps to perform Outbound processes, Picking by Warehouse order using RFUI 

Step1: 

Create Standard order using the Transaction code VA01

 

 

Step2: 

Create Outbound delivery:

 

Outbound delivery was distributed to EWM

 

 

Step3:

Created Warehouse task and warehouse order

Step4:

Performing picking by WO in RFUI

 

 

Provide the warehouse order number

 

 

 

 

The picking by warehouse order is confirmed.

 

Picking completed.

 

Conclusion: 

Steps to perform Outbound processes, Picking by Warehouse order using RFUI is provided.

entry.title: Re: Inner Joins is not working Composite Provider after U...

Hi

There is limitations in composite provider that inner join cannnot be used on top of left outer join.

entry.title: Re: ASSERTION_FAILED or FAA_CMP Errors in tcode AJAB and OAAQ and FAA_CMP and OAAR

If your closed Fiscal year is 2022 then the transfer date should be 01.01.2023 .

entry.title: Re: SAP Ariba integration with SAGE ERP (Third Party)
entry.content: You can use any middleware which can invoke WebServices using SOAP format. There are webservices available in Sourcing and you can integrate with any system.

Hi

Use Temporal join in composite for time dependent master data join.

Using Temporal Joins | SAP Help Portal

Regards,

Giri

I put type as HTTP, URL as http://192.168.0.18:8000, proxy type as Internet, Authentication as  BasicAuthentication with my SAP trial account userid and password. While checking for connection message shows " could not check at the moment, Please try again later".

Could any one help me. Other wise how to contact support expert for trial account users?

entry.title: Re: G/L account 1001 cannot be used (please correct)Messa...
entry.content:
Hi,

Can you follow below steps

1.Identify Field Status Group: Use T-code FSOO to identify the Field Status group for the GL account.
2.Verify Field Status Group Configuration: In T-code OBC4, verify and decide if fields should be Suppress, Required, or Optional Entry as required .
3.Adjust Cost Center Settings: In T-code OME9, adjust settings to define if the cost center should be Mandatory, Optional, Display, or Hidden, based on business needs.
4.Ensure Consistency: Ensure configurations in both T-codes are consistent for seamless GL postings.

Thanks,

Rajesh Reddy

entry.title: Re: Final settlement still credit customer after accrual...
entry.content:
Thank you very much @MichaelWerner for your answer.

As per your suggestion I have added a discount condition type to the settlement pricing procedure and it works.

entry.title: Re: SAC ADDIN EXCEL - Locked menu
entry.content:
@freddelain 

Could you check what is your role assigned. Does it have below permission for the Add-in object.

 

I think an Update permission will be required to edit other persons workbook.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD_OFFICE/2b73edbc8f8f4189a36bc7a2e038185c/9e5d840a4be94cb0a4ff9ddb174a08f9.html

 

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Link date to datefrom and dateto in calculation view
entry.content:
Hi,

Technology: SAP BW Calculation View in Eclipse

I have in master data the fields DATEFROM and DATETO and want to link it to CALMONTH/ FISCPER in transactional data. I think, the best way is to create a range with DATEFROM and DATETO and then check, if CALMONTH/ FISCPER is in the range, but I don't know how to create a range / interval. I also don't have the data type Array. 

Do you have an idea?

entry.title: Re: FS Items and GL Account Mapping Table into SAC
entry.content:
@sachinagarwal_680 

GL Accounts to FS Items mapping is done in SAP GR. You can import the FS Items from GR into SAC dimension using the Odata service API_GRMASTERDATA_SRV. I am not sure if you need the GL Account also, if yes you would need another dimension for that and use the OData service API_GLACCOUNTINCHARTOFACCOUNTS_SRV. The mapping between GL to FS Item will not be available in SAC unless you then create an attribute in GL Account dimension in SAC and map FS Items manually as you uploaded in GR.

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Not able to connect to system while creating a projec...
entry.content:
Hi,
Please make sure that your destination authentication is correctly defined.
You can also use the SAP Business Application Studio guided answers page: https://gad5158842f.us2.hana.ondemand.com/dtp/viewer/#/tree/2827/actions/41344:41346:57775/?version=current

(Please make sure to follow all steps, starting the destination configuration checks).

There is no requirement to login to CF when accessing SAP BTP destinations from SAP Business Application Studio.

Thanks,

Rotem

entry.title: SAP Datasphere Replication between Tenants
entry.content:
Hello Specs !

I have two tenants for Datasphere a Production and Test Systems.

I've been asked to migrate data from Prod To TEST for some testing purpose so I've created a space and mapped the Prod System from Test (also added the system to whitelist).
All in all I've a successfuly connection created with an Analysis user ready to use replication flow. 

My Problem is that when I try to run a replication flow the replication flow run on error.

The error messages like this :  insufficient privilege or this "Source setup failed due to the following error: Failed to prepare source replication artifacts due to database error. SAP DBTech JDBC: [258]: insufficient privilege: Detailed info for this error can be found with guid '40F6D4EB4460104A957A47ED58309D7E'. Please refer to SAP Note 2980489 to know which privilege is missing for the current user. Contact the system administrator for the HANA system to address the issue and then resume the replication task. CorrelationId: fa46e992-1f9a-4634-1900-daa69d271c86@26-Jan-2025 06:59:49."
So think I have issue with the user.

I've used call SYS.GET_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE_ERROR_DETAILS (<guid>, ?) but I couldn't find any error message there. And although I am system owner I cannot seems to see any option to grant higher rights for analysis user (at least I cannot find) so I am clueles how to grant the necessary rights or the necessary users for migrate data from one tenant to another)

Could you please help me out how to create user that has necessary right or grant him the necessary rights.

entry.title: Re: Integrate SAP Task Center with SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content: Hi @dhruba_sap , I am facing some issues and tasks are not populated after the configuration even through my task center admin tile is showing all the connections are OK. Is there  anything else configuration which do we need to do on s4hana system????

entry.title: Romanian New Employer Contribution Report - January 2025
entry.content:
There has been introduced new report for ER Contribution calculation that replace the report HROCBT01. For more details, please see the document attached to note 3317902.

There is still necessary to run the report HROCERC0 for the D112 purposes (to fill in the T7ROER cluster with some amounts at ER level). We will plan to remove this report completely in future (move the functionality directly to the D112 report).

entry.title: Re: Build Apps - Add Integration - BTP Destinations - Fet...
entry.content:
@GauthamV 

Go to your BTP Trial account and Destination (left side menu) where you need to configure your API endpoint or RFC ...  you can find the below links which are example to destination but if you don't find in those links .. you can search in developers.sap.com .. there are 100+ tutorials where you can find and learn and build on your own.

 

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-apps-cap-expose.html

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-cf-create-destination.html

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-apps-s4hana-crud.html

entry.title: How to display material entity data of one standard UIBB in another standard UIBB?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

While creating a material, I would like to know as how I can display the material entity data of one UIBB in another UIBB.

Characteristic value(Characteristics) in Material description(Descriptions).

 

Could you please help me on this.

Thanks in advance.

 

 

 

regards

Chandra Kishore.

entry.title: SAP 消息号 SGT_01 106
entry.content: SAP 消息号 SGT_01 106

entry.title: Live Session on SAP Next Generation Aftermarket Cloud!
entry.content:
Who Should Attend? 
This session is ideal for automotive consultants, industry experts, and professionals looking to enhance their knowledge and stay updated on the latest trends and technologies in the automotive aftermarket.
 
Why Attend?
The experts will highlight the importance of technological disruption, environmental changes, and rising competitiveness as drivers for transformation. They will cover trends and strategies such as optimized operations, sustainable logistics, and the integration of network-aware applications. The goal is to enable aftersales to become an intelligent, sustainable enterprise.
 
Date: 2025-02-19 Wednesday
Time: 10:00 - 11:00 am CET (GMT+1)
Speakers: Frank Globisch, Markus Kraemer
Register now: https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/next-generation-aftermarket-cloud
 
What to Expect:
  • Automotive Strategic Priorities: Learn about the key strategic priorities driving the automotive industry, including servitization, customer experience, software-defined vehicles, sustainability, and resilient supply chains.
  • Trends & Strategies: Discover the key drivers of transformation in the aftermarket industry, including technological disruption, environmental changes, and rising competitiveness.
  • Fulfillment & Execution: Learn about the highlights in fulfillment and execution, and how SAP's Aftermarket Cloud can optimize your operations.
  • Aftermarket Planning: Gain insights into advanced planning techniques and how AI and machine learning can enhance your planning processes.
  • Use Cases: Explore real-world use cases and success stories from leading automotive com
The presentation focuses on the Next Generation Aftermarket Cloud by SAP, highlighting its strategic priorities and innovative solutions for the automotive industry. Key areas covered include:
  1. Automotive Strategic Priorities:
    • Servitization: Transitioning from product-based to service-based business models.
    • Customer Experience: Enhancing customer satisfaction and loyalty.
    • Software-Defined Vehicles: Integrating software to improve vehicle functionality.
    • Sustainability: Focusing on sustainable business practices.
    • Resilient Supply Chain and Smart Manufacturing: Creating flexible and efficient supply chains.
  2. Focus on Aftermarket – Customer Support:
    • Customer Centricity: Providing a 720-degree view of the vehicle and customer.
    • Connected Vehicle: Leveraging telematics and vehicle data.
    • Predictive & Proactive Customer Care: Using AI for predictive analysis and customer interaction.
    • Circular Economy: Enabling automated decision-making for parts exchange and second-life parts.
  3. Reinvent Aftersales:
    • Next Gen. Aftermarket Parts Management: Capturing and managing demand across the OEM network.
    • AI Optimized Forecasting: Using AI algorithms for inventory planning and real-time execution.
    • Flexible Global Network Structure: Adapting to evolving network structures and optimizing inventory.
  4. Ever-modern SAP Aftermarket System:
    • AI Factory: Utilizing AI and machine learning for long-term forecasting and automation.
    • Generative AI Hub: Leveraging generative AI for innovative solutions.
    • Quantum Computing for Logistics: Exploring quantum computing for logistics optimization.
How to Register?
Register now to secure your spot for this informative and engaging live session. Next Generation Aftermarket Cloud
 
Don't miss this opportunity to learn from industry leaders and gain valuable insights into the future of the automotive aftermarket. 
 
We look forward to seeing you there!

Grzegorz Posyniak
Global Learning Portfolio Manager for Industries,
SAP Product and Solution Learning, SIX Solution and Innovation Experience

entry.title: Re: #abap_trial: Container does not start properly
entry.content: You need to check if your licenses are still valid. Please refer to the instructions on the documentation: abap-cloud-developer-trial .

• We have created a free style Fiori app using this Custom Business Object in Business Application Studio.
• We need to attach files to this Custom Business Object using Fiori app.

Regards,

Iftikhar

entry.title: Re: Limit number of tabs in web browser for Fiori
entry.content: Why?

entry.title: Process to Convert a Purchase Order to an inbound delivery with batch split
entry.content:
Purchase Order:
A purchase order in SAP is a formal request issued to a supplier for the delivery of goods or services. It includes detailed information such as item descriptions, quantities, and delivery terms. SAP helps track the status of purchase orders, ensuring timely procurement and accurate inventory management. 

 

Inbound Delivery:
An inbound delivery in SAP refers to the receipt of goods from a supplier or vendor. It contains details like shipping dates, transportation information, and the items being delivered. SAP enables efficient management of inbound deliveries, ensuring smooth goods receipt and accurate updates to inventory levels.

 

Batch:
A batch in SAP is a unique identifier assigned to a group of products produced or received together under the same conditions. Batches are used to track products for quality control, traceability, and compliance purposes. SAP allows the management and monitoring of batch attributes like expiry dates, production dates, and specific characteristics.

 

Batch Split:
Batch split in SAP refers to the process of dividing a batch into smaller quantities based on specific requirements such as shipping or storage needs. It is commonly used when products need to be allocated to different sales orders or storage locations. SAP facilitates batch splitting to optimize inventory management and ensure accurate tracking.

 

Steps to Convert a Purchase Order to an inbound delivery with batch split

Step1:

Create an Inbound delivery using the transaction code VL31N

 

Enter the Purchase Order details and the delivery date:

 

 

Step2:

Select the line item.

Enter the delivery quantity and vendor batch number.

Then save it.

 

Step3:

If you need to create multiple batches for the same vendor batch

Select the line item and click on the click on the batch split button.

 

Step4:

Considering you need product from multiple batches.

Provide the quantity and the supplier batch details

Then select all the line items and click on the batch creation button.

 

Step5:

Then provide the date of manufacturing and city of origin for all the new batches created.

Then click on the enter key.

 

Step6:

Then save, the inbound delivery will be created.

 

Conclusion:

Steps to Convert a Purchase Order to an inbound delivery with batch split is provided.

entry.title: Re: Build Apps - Add Integration - BTP Destinations - Fet...
entry.content:
pls check if you have updated the properties in your destination
@GauthamV 

Field Value

Appgyver.Enabledtrue
WebIDEEnabledtrue
HTML5.DynamicDestinationtrue

Integrate SAP Build Apps and SAP Build Process Aut... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Publishing Public Link
entry.content:
As far as I understand, deployed apps from SAP Build Apps -- deployed directly to the Work Zone approuter -- require SAP BTP authentication.

You could build an mtar and download it, and then modify it perhaps, and redeploy -- but my understanding is still the basis of the whole system is that SAP BTP must identify the user. More info perhaps here: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/build-and-deploy-web-applications

entry.title: Re: Build Apps - Add Integration - BTP Destinations - Fet...
entry.content: I have all these properties updated in the destination, still no luck.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Publishing Public Link
entry.content: I guess you are right about this. Because since the time I posted this here I have been trying to discover a way to bypass that but it does not seem possible.

entry.title: Re: Map time-dependant navigation attribute to transactio...
entry.content:
Hi Giri,

Temporal Join should work as you suggested, but my Composite Provider is Using Union. Union Node is not allowed as below. I got exactly this error message referring to Union node. I used a aDSO for the master data and derived 0calmonth from the date from and date to. I crated two fields for month from and month to. After that in join condition, I used 0calmonth coming with transaction data to join it in between month from and month to driven from master data. It worked like this. What I should try to create another Composite provider with temporal join and use this composite provider in the other composite provider which includes Union node. May be like that, we get rid of this limitation below of using union node. BR, Farouk

Prerequisites

The following restrictions apply:
  • Only InfoObjects and DataStore objects are supported.
  • Union nodes, aggregation, and projection are not allowed.

entry.title: Hidden fields within BDC, how to fill them ?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
I'm trying to create BDC for Transaction code (vd01), to create customer and using it in some programs.
During BDC recording, I noticed that there're some fields like [Building Code, mobile phone], they're existed in vd01 in case if I want to create customer using tcode [vd01] BUT not existed in BDC recording screens. 

What should I do to fill them with data in BDC recording? cause it's mandatory to fill them.
and Are there any other way to do that in more best practice way? 

#ABAP #BDC #DataMigration

entry.title: Re: issue with java script action in Build apps
entry.content:
I created this app to test your scenario, but with a simplified API.

In the first I fetch the Customers from Northwind, and return the first one (an object).

In the next, I take as the input the output of the previous JavaScript -- JSON object. ANd just to demonstrate I have an object and can work on it like an object, I display in the console the keys and values. I was also able to select specific fields in the object.

I can see the output in the console.

All this to demonstrate that the passing of JSON works between JS flow functions. Previously, we showed that you code also works.

Hope that helps.

entry.title: Re: Is Job Information History permission necessary for s...
entry.content: Thank you for your answers. It was helpful in convincing our business for providing the history data access in report.

entry.title: Re: How to hide the page Tab in Build apps Preview page
entry.content:
I believe the way you do this is by creating custom navigation (i.e., the default navigation does not support this).

Here is a blog by @marihirvi: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-blog-posts/create-on-brand-navigation-experiences-in-sap-build-apps-using-custom/ba-p/13617392

Essentially you could do 2 ways, both involve disabling the default navigation.

  • You can create a container the contains icons or text that are the navigation menu, and set visibility to true/false based on whatever you decide, for example, some page variable that tracks the user's role. We did this here in our challenge 2 years ago -- and you can even download it to see it: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-discussions/april-citizen-developer-challenge-sap-build-apps-task-0/m-p/13654312
  • You can use the marketplace component "Responsive navigation bar" with the custom navigation lists you can create (go to Settings > Navigation). Create a list for each role and then display that navigation based on the user's role. Essentially the same as the first but you hide/show an entire navigation bar. I don't like this one so much.

All of this is my understanding, but Mari will likely know more.

entry.title: Integration Suite: What is the advantage of using B2B TPM?
entry.content:
Hi,

I am learning this TPM component from Integration Suite, and when I learn it, there are some steps required to build it, such as Trading Partner Profile, Trading Agreement, Integration Advisor (MIG/MAG), etc.

When I look at this, I am trying to find what the advantage for using TPMs are compared to just build an iFlow for the integration? Is it the reusability of the components in TPM? That when we are trying to do integration with another party, assuming we agree to use the same message format, we can reuse the existing components from an existing TPM?

Thank you

Cloud Integration SAP Integration Suite

The right link is ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 Available Now - SAP Community.

entry.title: Re: Passing values in Input Parameters - Variables of Datasphere Model from Power BI
entry.content:
Hello @Jinal_Patel ,

are you using the so called "database analysis user" to read from an analytic model? because as far as I know the "normal" database user defined inside spaces can not read analytic models:

The column view generated by the analytic model can not be read with database user defined inside a space.

entry.title: Re: Need Alternative Option to CG3Z (Upload files to AL11 path)
entry.content: can you try changing the physical path behind the logical path EHS_FTAPPL_PATH_2 in Tcode FILE, if you are facing issue while uploading.

entry.title: How to set default value using @Consumption.filter.defaultValue (CDS RAP)
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Building a consumption view to display Supplier invoice data with workflow details. In that, the requirement is to build below inputs with a default value as 

1) Net value date : last day of the current month (calendar as VH)  

2) Fiscal year : Curent year (it has all years in the drop down/VH but must have the current year) 

3) PR block reason key : = !blank (it has all values but must be equal to blank) 

4) Document date : multiple range selection input with (first day of the current month and current day of the current month) 

Please help how to achieve it with the code. 

Thank you for your time and help here.

entry.title: Re: ECC-EWM-GTS Export Declaration “Before PGI in EWM” -B...
entry.content:
You can try customising PPF - Trigger based on picking complete status or any other status than PGI.
The PPF should trigger an interface and perform the GTS approval function required.

Other option is Invoice before GI.
